title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York / Tel Aviv HQ collaboration
description: Reporting to the CRO, this is a hands-on leadership role to build and scale the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling RevOps beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration expertise, and revenue analytics skills. Strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, plus experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ. Finout is a cloud cost management company backed by $85M+ and trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps with deep Salesforce expertise and experience scaling GTM operations aligns perfectly with Finout's need for a hands-on Head of RevOps. The Tel Aviv HQ collaboration and globally distributed team structure match his location flexibility and international experience.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations role and thought of you immediately—building the operational backbone for a $85M+ backed cloud cost management platform at scale is exactly the kind of strategic RevOps challenge you've excelled at. Your track record scaling GTM engines in B2B SaaS, combined with your Tel Aviv base, positions you uniquely for their NY/Tel Aviv collaboration model. I'd love to explore how your Salesforce expertise and cross-functional leadership could drive Finout's next growth phase.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager to redesign territory models and processes for global scalability. Responsibilities include partnering with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory compositions, analyzing historical performance and market potential to inform segmentation, and supporting GTM planning cycles with territory assignments and whitespace analysis. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool), and Salesforce proficiency. Experience with territory management tools (Fullcast, Pigment, Anaplan) is a plus. Salary range $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS background, and analytical skillset. Territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, and Salesforce proficiency are core strengths, though the role is more specialized in territory optimization than broader revenue operations.
ai_opener: Omri, your 15+ years scaling GTM operations across B2B SaaS makes you an ideal fit for Gong's RevOps Manager role—especially given your track record with pipeline forecasting and Salesforce optimization. Territory strategy and whitespace analysis directly leverage your CAC/LTV modeling expertise, and this remote position aligns perfectly with your geographic flexibility.

title: Manager, Revenue Operations
description: An operator role for someone who loves building and optimizing. You'll own and optimize core GTM systems and data stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Apollo, Clay, LeanData) to enable seamless workflows across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success. Partner with GTM leaders to design operational processes including forecasting, pipeline reviews, territory design, comp planning, lead routing, and attribution. Build and maintain dashboards providing real-time visibility into pipeline health, pacing, conversion rates, and sales efficiency metrics. Though Embrace is a remote company, collaboration and connection are key to its culture.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: core RevOps/GTM mandate aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years expertise, technical stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Apollo, LeanData) matches his strong admin background, and the operator/builder focus on forecasting, pipeline, and attribution directly leverage his CAC/LTV and pipeline forecasting strengths. Remote US role meets location flexibility.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager] – I saw the Manager, Revenue Operations role at Embrace and it immediately resonated. I've spent 15+ years architecting GTM systems and data stacks in B2B SaaS, and the specific mandate around Salesforce/HubSpot optimization, forecasting design, and pipeline visibility is exactly where I've driven the most operational leverage. I'd love to explore how we could build seamless GTM workflows at Embrace.

title: Director of Revenue Operations (RevOps)
description: A high-impact role designing, implementing, and improving the processes, systems, and insights that enable Enformion's go-to-market teams. You'll partner with the CRO, VP of Marketing, VP of Finance, and Customer Success leadership to align goals and drive predictable growth. Requires 10+ years of Revenue/Sales/Marketing Operations experience including 2+ years leadership, proven experience scaling RevOps in SaaS, strong command of HubSpot and GTM tech stack (Outreach, ZoomInfo), and deep understanding of SaaS metrics (ARR, ACV, retention, pipeline velocity). You'll manage and mentor a small RevOps team.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Director-level RevOps role aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise, leadership experience, and core competencies (HubSpot, SaaS metrics, GTM alignment). Remote US location removes any friction.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I've spent 15+ years building scalable RevOps functions in B2B SaaS, and Enformion's mission to drive predictable growth through process, systems, and insights resonates deeply with my background. I've led similar cross-functional alignment efforts (CRO, Marketing, Finance, CS) and scaled HubSpot ecosystems with GTM tech stacks—I'd welcome a conversation about how I can accelerate your RevOps maturity and pipeline velocity.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: As Warp's first Revenue Operations leader, you'll work across Sales, Marketing, Growth, and Product to ensure the funnel is predictable, efficient, and scalable. You'll inherit a stack built by a fractional RevOps agency, own it, and level it up. Responsibilities include ensuring inbound leads are routed, enriched, and followed up based on ICP and intent; building workflows to maximize conversion; owning lead scoring, assignment rules, SLAs, and pipeline hygiene; and operationalizing PQL scoring and usage-based triggers. Build the operational backbone that turns self-serve users into enterprise opportunities.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's core RevOps expertise—first RevOps leader role at growth-stage company, ownership of full funnel optimization, lead scoring, pipeline hygiene, and operationalizing PQL scoring are all signature strengths. Remote US/Canada location is acceptable and global-friendly.
ai_opener: Omri here—I noticed Warp is building out its first RevOps function to scale a self-serve-to-enterprise motion. That PQL-to-pipeline operationalization + lead scoring/assignment system is exactly what I've built at scale across B2B SaaS. Happy to discuss how to architect the stack your fractional agency started into something that compounds with your growth.

title: Revenue Operations Manager (Fractional)
description: RevOpsforce is a tech-enabled consulting firm that helps B2B SaaS companies scale and optimize revenue operations by streamlining tech stacks, automating workflows, and integrating data for full GTM alignment. This fractional RevOps role requires deep familiarity with Salesforce, Marketo, Cvent, Zoom, and leading SaaS ecosystem tools (Drift, Integrate). Excellent communication, leadership, and project management skills are needed, along with a demonstrated ability to thrive in ambiguous environments, work cross-functionally, and deliver results in distributed teams. The firm executes hands-on solutions rather than just advising.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps expertise, Salesforce/marketing automation proficiency, and B2B SaaS consulting experience. Fractional/project-based model suits senior executive seeking flexible engagement; only minor gap is Marketo/Cvent specialization versus HubSpot strength.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years scaling revenue operations in B2B SaaS. I've built and optimized the exact GTM stacks you help clients implement: Salesforce, marketing automation, pipeline forecasting, and cross-functional alignment. RevOpsforce's hands-on consulting approach resonates with me, and I'd be interested in exploring how my fractional expertise could help your clients accelerate their revenue operations maturity.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As Conveo scales toward 20x revenue in 2026, they need a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations. You'll replace patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—think lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines that work, onboarding flows that activate customers faster, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly.
score_reason: Strong GTM Engineer match with RevOps expertise and B2B SaaS background, but the role's emphasis on coding/development skills (building internal tools, AI agents, pipelines from scratch) is a moderate mismatch for a RevOps executive whose strength is in operations strategy and tool orchestration rather than full-stack engineering.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM and RevOps. I've built the exact systems Conveo needs: lead scoring models that actually predict, attribution pipelines that close the loop, and AI-powered workflows that replaced manual chaos with automation. With your research platform hitting 20x growth, I can architect the internal tools and automations that'll let your team scale without adding headcount.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Gradient Labs seeks a GTM Engineer with an enterprise SaaS operations background—5+ years in a RevOps, marketing ops, or sales ops role at an enterprise SaaS company, with experience running account-based motions. The ideal candidate has a systems-building instinct: thinking in workflows and turning half-built things into compounding systems. Fluency in tools like HubSpot, Clay, and Apollo is expected, treating tools as interchangeable. Genuine AI fluency is required—using AI daily, comfortable building small agents, with a fast pace, bias to action, and high agency and ownership.
score_reason: Exceptional match—GTM Engineer role aligns perfectly with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, enterprise SaaS background, and systems-building mindset. Remote UK location works globally, and the AI-native GTM focus matches his strong fit profile.
ai_opener: Gradient Labs caught my attention—you're building GTM as a compounding system with AI as a first-class tool, not an afterthought. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue operations and deep fluency in HubSpot, Salesforce, and AI-native workflows, I've spent the last few years thinking exactly like this: treating tools as interchangeable, automating the half-built things, and using AI daily to compress cycles. I'd like to explore how I can help you turn your GTM engine into something that compounds.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Redwood Software seeks a GTM Engineer to lead the design, implementation, and optimization of AI-driven tools, automations, and processes across the marketing department, ensuring the organization leverages AI safely and creatively from campaign automation to content production and analytics. title: Director, GTM Tech and Automation
description: Reporting to the VP Revenue Strategy & Operations, you'll build, automate, and operate the systems powering Ivo's entire go-to-market engine, AI-first. You'll own the full GTM systems layer: CRM architecture, cross-platform integrations, workflow automation, and data pipes connecting product usage to revenue. Requires 5–10 years in B2B SaaS GTM operations or revenue technology, 2+ years at a high-growth company (Series B–D), deep experience automating CRMs at scale, strong SFDC administration and SQL skills, and the ability to ship code fast using AI copilots.
